sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After 6 UPs, the squares are at different heights:
- Left square: rows 39-43, cols 19-23
- Right square: rows 44-48, cols 39-43
They moved at different rates! The left square moved 10 rows up (from 49 to 39), while the right square only moved 5 rows up (from 49 to 44). This means they don't always move together - they can get blocked independently.
Looking at the maze structure, the left square has a wider passage on its side (rows 19-43 has 10 Y wide passage on the left), while the right square's passage might be narrower. Let me check the right side more carefully.
Actually, looking at the maze again:
- Left side (cols 19-23): The passage on the left side is wider in rows 19-43 (10 Y wide)
- Right side (cols 39-43): The passage on the right side might be narrower
Wait, let me re-examine. The squares move independently based on what's blocking them. Let me look at what's at the current positions.
Let me first go back to the starting position and then explore more carefully. I need to understand the maze structure better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me look at the current state more carefully
# Left square at rows 39-43, cols 19-23
# Right square at rows 44-48, cols 39-43

# Let me check what's around the right square's position
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Right side area (cols 35-50, rows 40-55):")
for r in range(40, 56):
    row_str = ascii_lines[r][35:51]
    print(f"  {r}: {row_str}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! The right square at rows 44-48, cols 39-43 is in a narrow passage. Above it (rows 40-43), there are O's (orange) which are part of the wider passage. Below it (rows 49-53), there are B's (black), which is a wall.
The left square at rows 39-43, cols 19-23 is in a wider passage area.
Let me check the left side too to understand the full picture.
Actually, I think I need to understand the maze structure better. The squares seem to move independently, and they can get blocked by walls. Let me go back to the start and explore more systematically.
Let me first go back to the starting position by pressing DOWN enough times, then explore.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Go back to start position
# Left square at rows 39-43, needs to go to 49-53 (down 2 steps)
# Right square at rows 44-48, needs to go to 49-53 (down 1 step)
# But DOWN moves both, so let's just do DOWN 2 times
result = action(['DOWN', 'DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Sky blue: row=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}], col=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Sky blue: row=[49,53], col=[19,23]
  Sky blue: row=[54,58], col=[39,43]
